what_it_does: Defines a React Resizable component that lays out children as horizontal flex columns separated by draggable gutters, persists non-flex column widths to localStorage when a storageKey is provided, and exposes the component on window.Resizable.
observations: Uses React from the global React object with locally aliased hook names.
Treats cols entries with size "flex" as flexible columns and numeric sizes as fixed pixel widths.
Initial fixed sizes can be restored from localStorage under a cvstudio:resizable-prefixed key.
Dragging a gutter normally resizes the column to its left; if that column is the flex column, it resizes the column to the right with an inverted delta.
Mousemove and mouseup handlers are attached to window only while a drag is active.
Rendered gutters have role="separator" and aria-orientation="vertical".
The component assigns window.Resizable rather than exporting through module syntax.
standouts: Combines flex layout, draggable gutters, and localStorage-backed width persistence in a compact component.
Contains special handling for gutters adjacent to the flex column so fixed-width columns remain adjustable.
Uses child order and cols order as the layout contract.
conventions: Uses function components and React hooks.
Uses inline styles for the structural flex sizing rules.
Uses cv-resizable, cv-resizable-col, and cv-gutter class names for styling hooks.
Uses localStorage keys namespaced with cvstudio:resizable:.
Uses mouse events rather than pointer events.
concerns: The comment says exactly one column should be flex, but the component does not validate that condition.
Persisted sizes are restored by array index rather than by column id.
Keyboard resizing behavior is not implemented for the separator role.
Touch or pointer input is not handled.
The rendered child count can diverge from cols length without an explicit guard.
notes: A small global React layout primitive for persisted horizontal pane resizing.
questions: Should column ids be used when restoring persisted widths instead of numeric positions?
Should the component enforce or warn about the exactly-one-flex-column expectation?
Should gutters support keyboard, touch, or pointer interactions?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it provides a horizontal split layout with draggable gutters and persisted non-flex column sizes.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide reusable studio-style resizable columns without requiring a module bundler export or external layout library.
novelty: Uses globally aliased React hooks and window-level component exposure alongside localStorage-backed pane sizes.
contribution: Provides a reusable horizontal pane-resizing behavior with persisted fixed-width columns, gutter interaction semantics around a flex column, and simple styling hooks for integration into a larger interface.
summary_for_embedding: A React component that renders children as horizontally resizable flex columns with mouse-driven gutters and optional localStorage persistence for fixed column widths.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for creating a compact persisted split-pane layout control for a studio-like interface.

symbols.does: Renders a horizontal resizable column layout around its children.
symbols.description: This component derives initial column sizes from the cols prop, optionally restores fixed widths from localStorage, manages active drag state, and renders child panes with gutters between them. It persists size changes when storageKey is present and exposes structural class names plus inline flex sizing.

symbols.does: Handles mouse movement during an active gutter drag and updates one column width.
symbols.description: This drag handler computes horizontal delta from the drag start point, chooses the affected fixed-size column, applies inverted movement when the left column is flex, clamps the result to the column min and max, and stores the new size in component state.
This mouseup handler clears the drag state. Clearing drag state causes the effect cleanup to remove the window-level mouse handlers.

symbols.does: Starts a gutter drag from a mouse down event.
symbols.description: This function prevents the default mouse behavior and records the gutter index, initial pointer X coordinate, and a snapshot of current sizes. That snapshot is then used by the move handler to calculate width changes relative to the drag start.
